exemplar.title: To what extent has social media marketing influenced McDonaldâs sales revenue?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent has social media marketing influenced McDonaldâs sales revenue?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clear linkage of âcreativityâ to McDonaldâs TikTok campaigns (Grimace Shake, BTS meal)
Use of concrete metrics (followers, view counts) to illustrate creative impact
Strong organisational connection throughout most sections
weaknesses: Analysis of creativity not methodically woven through every tool/theory section
Lacks an explicit academic definition or operationalisation of âcreativityâ
Limited engagement with financial statements to deepen concept integration
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strand assessments place the work in the 4-mark band: it shows strong understanding and clear analysis of the key concept with partial integration across the IA. The student offers several powerful examples yet stops short of fully embedding creativity in every analytical section, justifying a 4/5.

strengths: Includes exactly three supporting documents, meeting IA requirements
Selection covers a range of campaign examples (BTS meal, Grimace trend, LinkedIn commentary)
weaknesses: Documents are mostly secondary commentaries lacking primary data depth
Inconsistent citation style and missing access dates reduce credibility
Limited analytical richness and range of perspectives
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Quantity is adequate (3 documents) but their relevance and depth are only marginally developed. The documents support the narrative but lack primary analytics or robust financial data, aligning with the 2-mark band for âgenerally relevant but lacking depth.â

strengths: Inclusion of an Ansoff Matrix demonstrates awareness of strategic frameworks
Use of generic SMM analysis indicates recognition of analytical tools
weaknesses: Only two tools/theories selected, below the expected portfolio
Application is superficial: limited quadrant discussion and no detailed metrics
Relevance to the research question is only loosely demonstrated
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands rate at band 2: some tools are selected and superficially applied, but the portfolio is too limited and the relevance to the research question not sufficiently clear, warranting 2/4.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Links specific campaigns to engagement spikes and infers sales growth
Provides several relevant arguments addressing how SMM influences revenue
weaknesses: Data selection is superficial and drawn largely from popular media
Revenue figures are asserted rather than rigorously analysed
Some calculations (e.g., reach percentages) lack precision
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The IA moves beyond limited analysis by connecting campaigns to sales impacts, meriting band 3 for âsome analysis of the research question.â However, data use remains superficial, preventing a higher mark.

strengths: Conclusion explicitly restates and answers the research question
Summarises key findings in a clear closing section
weaknesses: Consistency with evidence is only partial; assertions arenât always tied to specific data
Reuses general statements without reinforcing them with key statistics
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the conclusion directly addresses the research question (band 3), its linkage to the evidence is incomplete (band 2). Overall, the partial consistency aligns best with 2/3.

strengths: Logical structure throughout with clear sections (TOC, methodology, analysis, conclusion)
Research question is prominently stated and guides the flow
weaknesses: Duplicate conclusion headings create minor confusion
Some section imbalances and missing page numbers in the TOC
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The IA consistently follows an appropriate structure with only minor organisational issues, matching the top band descriptor and earning 2/2.

strengths: All required IA elements are present (title page, word count, tables, citations, bibliography)
Table of Figures and TOC enhance navigation
weaknesses: Typographical errors in citations and misplaced parentheses
URLs split across lines and missing retrieval dates
Inconsistent citation formatting and missing page numbers
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All presentation requirements are met, justifying full marks, though small formatting and consistency issues remain. 2/2.

strandSelections.reasoning: A logical structure is evident (TOC, methodology, analysis, conclusion). Minor issuesâduplicate conclusion heading and some section imbalanceâdo not derail overall coherence. This merits the âappropriate structure throughoutâ band.

Sources are clearly connected to the topic (case study on BTS meal, article on Grimace trend, LinkedIn commentary) yet offer limited depthâlargely secondary commentary rather than robust financial data. The range of perspectives and analytical richness is modest, aligning with the âgenerally relevant but lack depthâ descriptor.

The student includes a title page, word count, table of contents, figures table, in-text citations and a bibliography. All required IA elements appear to be present, justifying full marks for presentation.
Exactly three supporting documents are appended, meeting the 3-5 requirement. This places the work in the middle of the scale where quantity is acceptable but not extensive.

Application is mostly descriptive. The Ansoff Matrix is used to label SMM as market penetration with minimal risk, but no detailed quadrant evaluation or financial modelling is provided. Discussion of engagement metrics is loosely linked to revenue without quantitative substantiation. This represents superficial relevance in line with the 2-mark criterion.

Data are present (follower counts, TikTok views, growth percentages) but largely drawn from popular media rather than primary financial statements; revenue figures are asserted rather than analysed. Selection is therefore superficial, placing the strand in the 2-mark band.

The narrative does attempt to answer how SMM influences sales, linking specific campaigns to engagement spikes and inferring sales growth. While the reasoning is not rigorous, it exceeds âlimited analysisâ and provides several relevant arguments, fitting the descriptor âsome analysis of the research questionâ at 3 marks.

The final section explicitly restates the research question and claims a positive influence, thereby clearly answering the question. Although depth is limited, an explicit answer meets the top band descriptor for this strand.

Only two formal tools are declared: generic âSMM analysisâ and the Ansoff Matrix. This constitutes âsome tools and theories selectedâ but is clearly below an adequate portfolio expected for HL, matching the 2-mark band.

Conclusions indicate that SMM has ârevolutionisedâ revenue and are broadly consistent with the qualitative evidence presented. Because the underlying data foundation is thin, consistency is partial rather than complete, aligning with the 2-mark level.

The investigation remains firmly centred on McDonaldâs. Specific metrics (92.5 million followers, 1 870 % follower spike, 689 million TikTok views) and concrete campaigns (Grimace Shake, BTS meal) are analysed to illustrate effects on sales. Although integration is not fully sustainedâfinancial statements are mentioned but not examined in depthâthe linkage between the organisation and the argument is strong and evident across most sections, warranting the âstrong analysis with partial integrationâ band.

The student consistently links the key concept of creativity to McDonaldâs social-media activity, citing the playful tone on TikTok, the user-generated Grimace trend and collaborations such as the BTS meal. These examples show a firm grasp of how creative messaging differentiates the brand. However, the discussion is concentrated in a few discrete paragraphs and is not woven methodically through every section (e.g., the Ansoff analysis only briefly references creativity). This reflects a strong, but only partially integrated, treatmentâbest matched to the 4-mark descriptor.

annotations.comment: Most supporting documents are secondary commentaries; including primary platform analytics or McDonaldâs internal data would enhance depth.
The research question is clear and focused on assessing SMMâs impact on McDonaldâs sales revenue, providing a strong foundation for the investigation.
Defining 'creativity' with an academic reference or operational definition would deepen the integration of the key concept throughout the analysis.
Consider specifying a timeframe or particular platforms within the research question to enhance precision (e.g., TikTok and Instagram between 2021â2024).
Duplicate âConclusionâ entries in the Table of Contents create confusion; remove one to streamline navigation.
Several ToC entries lack page numbers; including these would improve usability and meet presentation standards.
Including both a Table of Contents and a Table of Figures enhances document navigation and demonstrates good presentation practice.
The background section effectively outlines McDonaldâs scale and industry position, setting context for the study.
The citation âMontevirgen and Ashburnâ lacks a publication year; ensure consistent APA/MLA formatting for in-text citations.
Link the concept of creativity explicitly in the background by illustrating how it underpins specific McDonaldâs campaigns.
The methodology lists âSMM analysisâ without defining its framework or criteria; clarify the analytical steps and metrics to be used.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Specify which financial metrics (e.g., sameâstore sales, quarterly revenue growth) and report sections will be analyzed from McDonaldâs financial statements.
The use of generic industry figures (e.g., 77% of companies use SMM) is interesting but detracts from a McDonaldâsâspecific focus; prioritize brandâspecific data.
Comparing McDonaldâs follower count to Starbucks provides useful context and strengthens the argument about market leadership.
The #GrimaceShake view metric is compelling, but the claim of reaching 70% of TikTok users overlooks repeat views; refine the calculation.
The Grimace Shake example powerfully illustrates creativity in McDonaldâs SMM; it is one of the chapterâs strongest case studies.
The Ansoff Matrix is introduced but only the market penetration quadrant is discussed; expand to other quadrants or justify their exclusion.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Including the full matrix visually demonstrates breadth of strategic options; consider annotating each quadrant with SMM examples.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The inclusion of the Ansoff Matrix demonstrates awareness of strategic frameworks and enriches the toolset.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Great acknowledgement of SMM limitations; you could strengthen by proposing concrete riskâmitigation tactics for negative campaigns.
Typographical error around the citation â(Supporting Document) 1)â disrupts flow; correct parenthesis placement.
The conclusion explicitly answers the research question and reiterates key findings, fulfilling the criterion for closure.
The conclusion reuses assertions without tying back to specific data points; integrate key statistics (e.g., 1 870 % spike) to reinforce evidence consistency.
Duplicate âConclusionâ heading appears twice; remove one instance to improve structure.
Three supporting documents meet the IA requirement; good selection for a range of campaign examples.
Supporting documents lack uniform citation style and access dates, reducing their credibility; apply consistent formatting.
Bibliography includes a useful range of sources from academic texts to industry analytics, supporting a balanced perspective.
URLs split across lines and missing retrieval dates in the bibliography hinder readability; ensure each entry is complete and properly formatted.
